source: Numerics/Mathematics/Optimization/Global/DifferentialEvolution.cs; Test_DifferentialEvolution.cs exists but only exercises TestFunctions.cs objectives (Booth, FXYZ, McCormick, ...) at loose 1E-4 tolerances, which this port's closed named-function registry deliberately does not re-port (see task brief). Every case here instead reuses the P3.3 numerical_derivative closed function registry ('quadratic', 'normal_loglik') so the same objective is implemented identically on both sides of the oracle gate.
reference: Numerics @ a2c4dbf. args = [fnId, direction, D, lower(D values), upper(D values), index] (see differential_evolution_best_value() in core/tests/test_fixtures.cpp and DifferentialEvolutionBestValue() in tools/oracle_emitter/Program.cs, which MUST implement this convention identically). fnId: 0 = 'quadratic' (f(x) = sum_i (x_i - i)^2, 0-based i), 1 = 'normal_loglik' (Normal(mu=x0, sigma=x1).LogLikelihood on the embedded 5-point sample {9,10,11,12,13}, mean 11) -- the exact same two named functions the numerical_derivative.json fixture uses. direction: 0 = Minimize(), 1 = Maximize(). index: 0..D-1 selects BestParameterSet.Values[index] (0-based); index == D selects BestParameterSet.Fitness. Every other DifferentialEvolution/Optimizer knob (PRNGSeed=12345, PopulationSize=10*D, Mutation=0.75, DitherRate=0.9, CrossoverProbability=0.9, MaxIterations=10000, AbsoluteTolerance/RelativeTolerance=1E-8, ReportFailure=true, ComputeHessian=true) is left at the library default, matching the only real call site this port has seen so far (MCMCSampler.cs's MAP init, a later task), which overrides only ReportFailure (irrelevant to the values asserted here -- see optimizer.hpp's file header for why hitting a Max*Reached cutoff is unaffected by ReportFailure's value). Both cases converge to OptimizationStatus.Success well inside the default MaxIterations budget (27 and 44 generations respectively), confirmed via a standalone C# harness against the real upstream/Numerics build before curating these values. Every expected value was generated by running these exact args through 'tools/oracle_emitter --dump' against the REAL C# DifferentialEvolution and confirming the printed actual reproduces via verify_oracles.py -- this is the primary fidelity gate for the port's random-draw-order transcription (stable-sort triple-index pick, jRand double comparison, dither draw order; see differential_evolution.hpp's header comment for the full hazard list). Tolerances (1e-6 abs) are calibrated generously above the sub-1e-9 cross-language floating-point noise actually observed (verified via the same standalone harness comparing C++ and C# to full double precision) -- tight enough that any genuine algorithmic divergence (e.g. a wrong draw order desyncing the two PRNG streams) would blow through it by many orders of magnitude, since DE's population would then land in a completely different region of the search space rather than merely accumulate rounding noise.
